description_long: This is a dark, roughly hemispherical bowl with a wide, open mouth and a slightly irregular rim. The surface is matte and textured with visible horizontal striations, likely from hand-forming or coiling techniques. The bowl appears to be made of fired clay or ceramic, with a dark brown to black coloration and some lighter patches and discolorations. There are visible cracks and a large break or hole near the base, indicating significant wear or damage. The interior is also dark and unglazed, matching the exterior in texture and color. The overall form is utilitarian, with no visible handles or decorative elements.
description: A dark, textured ceramic bowl with a wide mouth, visible cracks, and a large break near the base. The surface is matte and unadorned, showing signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: This view shows the bowl's rounded body, with visible horizontal striations and a lighter patch on the surface.
description_view_1: The bowl's curvature and wide opening are prominent here, with a mostly uniform dark surface and minimal visible damage.
description_view_2: The open mouth and interior of the bowl are clearly visible, showing the consistent dark coloration and rough texture.
description_view_3: This angle highlights a large break or hole near the base, as well as several cracks running up the side of the bowl.
attribute_context_of_use: holding or serving food, possibly for cooking or storage
